task_yaml.task:
I'm handing off a project to a new lead this week and I need a single
consolidated brief they can read on day one. The project lives across a
Notion page and its 8 sub-pages — grab everything with the workspace's
the Notion API skill to pull the page tree, then use the
knowledge-consolidation skill to roll it all into one document.
Start from this project page and authenticate with $NOTION_API_TOKEN:
https://www.notion.so/ClawBench-34808720dfd48081b333d54b7fc54025?pvs=12

Consolidate everything into /tmp_workspace/results/consolidated.md so
the new lead walks away knowing what we've decided so far, what action
items are outstanding with owner and due date, what open questions
still aren't resolved, what risks or concerns the team has surfaced,
and what's currently blocked or waiting on a dependency.

One thing to watch carefully: some decisions show up in more than one
page — proposed early in one page, revised in a later one, and finally
confirmed in a retro or follow-up page. For those, only the final form
belongs in the Decisions section, and the entry must explicitly note
that the earlier version is superseded so the new lead doesn't act on
stale guidance. Read all 8 pages before locking in the final form.

Format-wise:
- H1: project name
- H2: Meetings — dedup summary, grouped by month
- H2: Decisions — table of date, topic, decision, status, source-page-id
- H2: Action Register — owner, action, due date if stated, status/blocker, source-page-id
- H2: Open Questions — question, owner or pending owner, next step, source-page-id
- H2: Risks and Dependencies — risk/dependency, evidence, mitigation/next action, owner, source-page-id
- H2: References

Within Action Register, items that are blocked or waiting on a
dependency should be flagged in the status_or_blocker column, e.g.
"blocked: waiting on X", rather than just left as open — I want to be
able to see at a glance what's stuck and on what.

Also save two tracker files for follow-up:
- /tmp_workspace/results/decision_log.csv with columns date,topic,decision,status,source_page_id,source_excerpt
- /tmp_workspace/results/action_register.csv with columns owner,action,due_date,status_or_blocker,source_page_id

Preserve source page_ids in every section and tracker file so I can
cross-link back.
task_yaml.task_snapshot:
I'm handing off a project to a new lead this week and I need a single
consolidated brief they can read on day one. The project's Notion tree
— the main page plus its 8 sub-pages — is already exported to
/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/notion_snapshot.json, so read from
there. Do not attempt a live Notion API call in this run; the snapshot
is the canonical input. Use the workspace's notion-token-api skill for
the Notion data shape and the local-knowledge-consolidator skill to
roll everything into one document.

Consolidate everything into /tmp_workspace/results/consolidated.md so
the new lead walks away knowing what we've decided so far, what action
items are outstanding with owner and due date, what open questions
still aren't resolved, what risks or concerns the team has surfaced,
and what's currently blocked or waiting on a dependency.

belongs in the Decisions section, and the entry must explicitly note
that the earlier version is superseded so the new lead doesn't act on
stale guidance. Read all 8 pages before locking in the final form.

Format-wise:
- H1: project name
- H2: Meetings — dedup summary, grouped by month
- H2: Decisions — table of date, topic, decision, status, source-page-id
- H2: Action Register — owner, action, due date if stated, status/blocker, source-page-id
- H2: Open Questions — question, owner or pending owner, next step, source-page-id
- H2: Risks and Dependencies — risk/dependency, evidence, mitigation/next action, owner, source-page-id
- H2: References

Within Action Register, items that are blocked or waiting on a
dependency should be flagged in the status_or_blocker column, e.g.
"blocked: waiting on X", rather than just left as open — I want to be
able to see at a glance what's stuck and on what.

Also save two tracker files for follow-up:
- /tmp_workspace/results/decision_log.csv with columns date,topic,decision,status,source_page_id,source_excerpt
- /tmp_workspace/results/action_register.csv with columns owner,action,due_date,status_or_blocker,source_page_id

Preserve source page_ids in every section and tracker file so I can
cross-link back.
